entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench, a cereal crop evaluated for nitrogen requirements and utilization.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., a legume crop evaluated for nitrogen requirements and utilization, including nodulating and non-nodulating genotypes.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: Mineral nitrogen (N) and atmospheric nitrogen (N2) used as essential nutrients for plant growth.

entities.name: NITROGEN USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The measure of biomass produced per unit of nitrogen harvested.

entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting atmospheric N2 into ammonia, performed by nodulating groundnut genotypes.

entities.name: NITROGEN FERTILIZATION
entities.description: The application of nitrogen fertilizer to plants to observe effects on growth and yield.

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of the weight of the harvested grain to the total above-ground biomass.

entities.name: NITROGEN TRANSLOCATION TO SEEDS
entities.description: The movement of nitrogen from vegetative tissues to the seeds.

entities.name: NITRATE REDUCTASE ACTIVITY
entities.description: The enzymatic activity (NRA) responsible for reducing nitrate in the leaves.

entities.name: NITRATE CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of nitrate present in the leaf tissues.

entities.name: MICHAELIS-MENTON CONSTANT
entities.description: The Km value indicating the affinity of the nitrate reductase enzyme for its substrate.

relationships.description: utilizes mineral nitrogen and fixes atmospheric nitrogen
exhibits higher efficiency of 120 kg biomass/kg N
exhibits lower efficiency of 36 kg biomass/kg N
increases harvest index and nitrogen translocation to seeds
decreases harvest index and has variable effects on nitrogen translocation to seeds
shows higher activity compared to groundnut
shows lower activity and lower affinity for substrate
shows lower nitrate concentration in leaves
shows higher nitrate concentration in leaves
affinity is characterized by the Km value
